canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, misclassification is most commonly defined as an observable failure mode where a model produces an incorrect class label for a given input. This behavior is observed in various contexts, from text classification where an input is assigned to a wrong category, to vision-language models where it is framed as “tricking VLMs into misidentifying class labels” (Shadowcast: Stealthy Data Poisoning Attacks Against Vision-Language Models). A more specific variant is “triggered misclassification,” which describes incorrect classification specifically in response to inputs containing a backdoor trigger. The provided research identifies several conditions that can lead to this behavior. One paper reports that `Backdoor vulnerability` causes misclassification, noting that when a “poisoned trigger” is present, the model is induced to produce an adversarial response. Other work states that “even slight perturbations to input data can result in misclassification” (Text-Guided Attention is All You Need for Zero-Shot Robustness in Vision-Language Models). The behavior is operationalized by measuring the rate at which a model produces a targeted adversarial output or an incorrect label under these conditions.
papers: Confidence Elicitation: A New Attack Vector for Large Language Models
Re-Imagining Multimodal Instruction Tuning: A Representation View
Backdoor Attacks in Token Selection of Attention Mechanism
ICLShield: Exploring and Mitigating In-Context Learning Backdoor Attacks
Shadowcast: Stealthy Data Poisoning Attacks Against Vision-Language Models
Text-Guided Attention is All You Need for Zero-Shot Robustness in Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: An observable failure mode where the model produces an incorrect class label for a given input.
definitions.paper: Text-Guided Attention is All You Need for Zero-Shot Robustness in Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: Responding to an image with the wrong class label or identity, such as naming one person or object as another.
definitions.paper: Shadowcast: Stealthy Data Poisoning Attacks Against Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The observable behavior in which a model incorrectly classifies inputs that contain a backdoor trigger, producing a targeted adversarial output.
definitions.paper: Backdoor Attacks in Token Selection of Attention Mechanism

relationships.paper: Backdoor Attacks in Token Selection of Attention Mechanism
relationships.evidence: “backdoor attacks are particularly insidious: they introduce ‘poisoned triggers’ ... When a trigger is present, the model produces an adversarial response; otherwise, it behaves normally.”

icml25: Backdoor Attacks in Token Selection of Attention Mechanism
